steps.title: Identify the key claim about Pueblo sizes
steps.content: The passage states that while Laguna (~800 square miles) is exceptional, most Pueblo tribal nations are much smaller. Sandia (38.9) demonstrates this typical smaller scale.

steps.title: Verify which value fits this pattern
steps.content: From the table, Pueblo de Cochiti is 82.1 square miles—a value consistent with most Pueblos being much smaller than Laguna. The other choices either misidentify Pueblo de Cochiti's area or present a value contradicting the narrative. That matches choice C — C is correct.
